channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas lawmakers will debate saving the state’s water supply after key legislation is introduced
item.description: The state House and Senate have similar proposals to solve the state's water crisis, but there are stark differences on how to invest billions of dollars to resolve.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez And Jayme Lozano Carver, KPRC 2

item.title: Oversight Democrats launch review of crypto reserve 'poised to enrich' Trump
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are encouraging President Trump to abandon his plans for a crypto reserve, calling it a strategy to enrich himself at taxpayer expense.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Hear the death threats Republican senator received about Trump
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., his staff and family have been the target of extensive harassment and death threats, a memo released by his office on Thursday afternoon revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New poll details Americans' views on Trump and Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump's poll numbers are edging down since taking over the White House nearly eight weeks ago. And Americans appear to be giving DOGE's Elon Musk a thumbs-down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kuwait frees 10 jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released 10 American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies. “President Trump has made it a priority to bring home Americans detained overseas.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ drops fight against Texas political maps as Trump administration retreats from voting rights cases
item.description: The Biden administration had challenged a Republican-led redistricting plan that diluted the political power of minorities. Other legal challenges will continue.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Natalia Contreras, Votebeat And The Texas Tribune, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's pick for ambassador to Canada says it's a sovereign state. Trump wants it as a US state
item.description: President Donald Trump's choice to be U.S. ambassador to Canada on Thursday said that America’s northern neighbor is a sovereign state, showing some daylight with Trump on the issue. The president has insisted that Canada would be better off as the United States' newest member. Former Rep.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump brings birthright citizenship argument to Supreme Court
item.description: Trump signed the executive order narrowing birthright citizenship on his first day back in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Two billionaires that are villains of the left and right take the spotlight in a key Wisconsin race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — When Republicans are looking for a political bogeyman, they point to liberal megadonor George Soros. Democrats recently have been answering with a villain of their own: the world's wealthiest man and close adviser to President Donald Trump, Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump's steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Americans held captive by South American dictator symptom of deeper threat, says former Green Beret
item.description: Former U.S. Army Green Beret Jordan Goudreau is sounding the alarm about a deeper national security problem at play after the State Department revealed nine Americans are held hostage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Trump administration’s policies are affecting farmers
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about how President Trump’s tariffs and funding cuts could affect Minnesota farmers.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: The Trump administration demands UN agencies disclose any 'anti-American' ties
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Trump administration is demanding U.N. humanitarian agencies that receive or disburse U.S. funding fill out a questionnaire disclosing any ties to communism, socialism or anti-American beliefs, according to U.S. and U.N.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transgender lawmaker slams Republicans' 'bizarre' focus on culture wars
item.description: Rep. Sarah McBride, the first openly transgender member of Congress, accused Republicans of being "obsessed with culture war issues," saying the focus was "weird" and "bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top diplomats from G7 countries meet in Canada as Trump threatens more tariffs on US allies
item.description: LA MALBAIE, Canada (AP) — Top diplomats from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies gathered in Canada on Thursday as U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade and foreign policies have thrown the bloc’s once solid unity into disarray.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blue state's LGBT 'conversion therapy' ban violates Constitution, 'very easy case' for SCOTUS, says expert
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has agreed to take up a case challenging a Colorado law's ban on so-called "conversion therapy" for minors after it declined to hear a similar case last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security says it will no longer accept bank changes over the phone
item.description: Those who need to change their direct deposit information with Social Security will have to do it online or in-person.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: The 2024 election in Georgia's Fulton County was 'organized and orderly,' independent monitors say
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia's Fulton County, where past elections have been marred by long lines, slow precinct reporting and even false claims of voter fraud, conducted an “organized and orderly” election last year, a team of independent monitors found.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Activist Mahmoud Khalil's arrest 'deeply concerning': Ex-NYC mayor
item.description: "You don't get arrested for disagreeing with the U.S. government," said former NYC Mayor Bill de Blasio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: UN chief in Bangladesh for a first-hand look at Rohingya camps as he pushes for aid
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres is getting a first-hand look at the situation of more than 1 million Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh as fears mount of serious aid cuts across the world following Washington's decision to shut down USAID operations.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House withdraws CDC director nomination
item.description: The White House has withdrawn the nomination of Dave Weldon, a former Republican congressman from Florida, to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Judge orders Trump to reinstate probationary workers let go in mass firings
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has President Donald Trump’s administration to rehire thousands of probationary workers let go in mass firings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats look to save face in shutdown battle
item.description: Senate Democrats say privately that they will not allow the government to shut down Saturday, despite growing pressure from activists and liberal lawmakers who want them to kill a GOP-crafted six-month stopgap spending bill. Senate Democratic sources say Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat trying again to secure discounts for all Nevadans on 10 expensive prescription medications
item.description: Prescription medication costs are forcing some hard choices for many Nevadans. Two years ago, a proposal that offered hope sailed through the Democrat-majority Nevada Legislature only to be vetoed by Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota House is tied after DFL wins special election. Here’s what changes
item.description: DFLer David Gottfried won the suburban Ramsey County seat and is expected to be sworn in to the House of Representatives next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Jeanne Shaheen to retire, setting up battle for New Hampshire Senate seat
item.description: “It was a difficult decision, made more difficult by the current environment in the country — by President Trump and what he’s doing right now,” said Shaheen, who is 78.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Digital license plate bill could include a way for Nevadans to pay registration in installments
item.description: GPS features, no stickers and possibly a way to pay your vehicle registration in installments. Those were some of the reasons that digital license plates might get support at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U of M says it's committed to combating antisemitism amid federal probes
item.description: The University of Minnesota says it’s working to address antisemitism on campus, amid federal investigations spurred by protests against Israel. Recent federal actions are raising alarms for pro-Palestinian and civil rights advocates, who say it’s a violation of free speech.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: UNLV, UNR funding would dip under new formula presented to Nevada lawmakers
item.description: A rebuilt funding formula for the state's colleges and universities became a target for officials Tuesday during a budget hearing in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada sex trafficking law 'nearly a parking ticket' for johns; higher fines, more exposure proposed
item.description: Getting tough on the customers who pay for sex is the best way to attack human trafficking, according to a woman who told her story to lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
